attractions.description: The famous active volcano that erupted catastrophically in 1980, now a fascinating monument with lava flows, blast zones still recovering, and Johnston Ridge Observatory with views into the crater. Adds about 2.5 hours to the drive.

attractions.name: Pike Place Market
attractions.description: One of America's oldest continuously operated public farmers markets (1907), perched above Elliott Bay. Watch fishmongers throw whole salmon, browse local produce and artisan goods, and find the original Starbucks on the main arcade.

attractions.name: Space Needle and Chihuly Garden and Glass
attractions.description: Seattle's futuristic 605-foot Space Needle offers 360-degree views from the rotating glass floor observation deck. Adjacent Chihuly Garden and Glass showcases the largest collection of Dale Chihuly glass art in the world.
attractions.hours: Space Needle open daily 11am-9pm (extended on weekends); Chihuly 11am-7pm
attractions.entranceFee: Space Needle $35 adults; Chihuly $29 adults; combo tickets available

name: Gulf Coast Loop
subtitle: Dallas → Houston → New Orleans → Gulf Beaches → Florida → Home

budget.label: Budget
budget.includes: Budget motels, po-boys & local spots, free beaches

midrange.label: Mid-range
midrange.includes: Mid-range hotels, NOLA restaurant scene, major attractions

premium.label: Premium
premium.includes: Boutique hotels, Commander's Palace, VIP jazz tours

transportNotes: ~2225 miles total loop. Hurricane season (Jun–Aug): check forecasts if traveling in summer. NOLA parking can be expensive — park once and walk/rideshare.
highlights: Explore New Orleans' French Quarter, Garden District, and live music scene on Frenchmen Street
Visit the National WWII Museum, one of the top-rated museums in the United States
Navigate Louisiana bayous on a swamp tour to see alligators in their natural habitat
Swim and relax on the Emerald Coast's iconic white-sand beaches at Pensacola and Destin
Explore NASA Johnson Space Center in Houston with tram tours of the active campus
Experience Tampa's Ybor City Latin culture and Clearwater Beach sunsets
Optional: extend to Orlando's theme parks or Kennedy Space Center

hotels.name: Hotel ZaZa Houston Museum District
hotels.notes: Boutique luxury near Museum District and Hermann Park

hotels.name: Marriott Houston Downtown
hotels.notes: Reliable mid-range, convenient to Space Center shuttle

hotels.name: La Quinta Downtown
hotels.notes: Budget-friendly in downtown

attractions.name: Space Center Houston
attractions.description: The official visitor center of NASA's Johnson Space Center, featuring 400+ space artifacts, three flown spacecraft, a moon rock display, and tram tours onto the active NASA campus including historic Mission Control. Plan at least 4-5 hours.
attractions.hours: Open daily 9am-6pm (extended summer hours); closed Thanksgiving and Christmas
attractions.entranceFee: $30-$40 adults online, $25-$35 children ages 4-11 online; under 4 free

attractions.name: Houston Museum District
attractions.description: Nineteen museums within a 1.5-mile radius, including the free Houston Museum of Natural Science, the Museum of Fine Arts Houston, and the Children's Museum. The Natural Science Museum has a famous gem and mineral hall.
attractions.entranceFee: Varies; Museum of Natural Science $25 adults; several are free

attractions.name: Galveston Island and Pleasure Pier
attractions.description: A 30-minute drive south of Houston, this barrier island offers Victorian architecture, Gulf of Mexico beaches, and the Pleasure Pier amusement park built over the water. Walk the historic Strand District for restaurants and shops.
attractions.hours: Pleasure Pier open daily in summer 11am-10pm; reduced off-season hours
attractions.entranceFee: Pier access free; rides $15-$40 per person

roadTripDays.tips: Avoid New Orleans during Mardi Gras (Feb-March) and Jazz Fest (late April-May) unless you have hotel reservations booked 6-12 months in advance; prices triple

hotels.name: Hotel Monteleone
hotels.notes: Historic landmark on Royal Street; famous Carousel Bar

hotels.name: Omni Royal Orleans
hotels.notes: Elegant French Quarter hotel with rooftop pool

hotels.name: Ace Hotel New Orleans
hotels.notes: Boutique in the Warehouse District; great music scene nearby

hotels.name: India House Hostel
hotels.notes: Best-rated hostel in NOLA; private rooms available

attractions.name: Tabasco Factory and Avery Island
attractions.description: The famous hot sauce has been made on this private salt dome island since 1868. Take a factory tour, walk the jungle garden, and visit the bird sanctuary. About 2 hours southwest of New Orleans on I-90.

attractions.name: Vermilionville Heritage Park, Lafayette
attractions.description: A living history museum on a 23-acre Cajun and Creole village recreating 18th and 19th century Louisiana life. Cooking demonstrations, live Zydeco music, and local artisans. A great introduction to Cajun culture before reaching New Orleans.

attractions.name: Oak Alley Plantation
attractions.description: An antebellum sugar plantation on the River Road between Baton Rouge and New Orleans, famous for its 300-year-old tunnel of 28 oak trees leading to the Greek Revival mansion. Thoughtfully interpreted to acknowledge the enslaved people who built the estate.

roadTripDays.tips: The best time for Gulf Coast beaches is April-May and September-October to avoid summer heat, humidity, and peak crowds

hotels.name: Hotel Monteleone
hotels.notes: Historic landmark on Royal Street; famous Carousel Bar

hotels.name: Omni Royal Orleans
hotels.notes: Elegant French Quarter hotel with rooftop pool

hotels.name: Ace Hotel New Orleans
hotels.notes: Boutique in the Warehouse District; great music scene nearby

hotels.name: India House Hostel
hotels.notes: Best-rated hostel in NOLA; private rooms available

attractions.name: French Quarter and Jackson Square
attractions.description: The heart of New Orleans: cast-iron balconies, Bourbon Street, Royal Street antique shops, and the 1718 St. Louis Cathedral facing Jackson Square. Visit the French Market, try beignets at Cafe du Monde (open 24 hours), and take a free walking tour of the Quarter.

attractions.name: Preservation Hall
attractions.description: An intimate jazz performance venue in the French Quarter that has preserved traditional New Orleans jazz since 1961. Standing-room-only shows in a rustic hall. Lines form 30-40 minutes before show time. One of the most authentic live music experiences in America.
attractions.entranceFee: $25 general standing admission (door, cash); $40-$50 advance reserved seats online

attractions.name: Swamp Tour in the Bayou
attractions.description: Pontoon boat tours departing from New Orleans into the surrounding cypress swamps to see American alligators, great blue herons, roseate spoonbills, and ancient tupelo trees draped in Spanish moss. Multiple operators offer 2-hour tours, some with transportation from the French Quarter.

roadTripDays.tips: Hurricane season runs June 1 through November 30; check weather forecasts closely if traveling June-October, especially the Gulf Coast

hotels.name: Hotel Monteleone
hotels.notes: Historic landmark on Royal Street; famous Carousel Bar

hotels.name: Omni Royal Orleans
hotels.notes: Elegant French Quarter hotel with rooftop pool

hotels.name: Ace Hotel New Orleans
hotels.notes: Boutique in the Warehouse District; great music scene nearby

hotels.name: India House Hostel
hotels.notes: Best-rated hostel in NOLA; private rooms available

attractions.name: Garden District and St. Charles Streetcar
attractions.description: Ride the historic St. Charles Avenue streetcar ($1.25) to the Garden District, lined with antebellum mansions, Spanish moss-draped oaks, and celebrities' homes. Walk past Anne Rice's former home and Lafayette Cemetery No. 1 (open daily, free).

attractions.name: National WWII Museum
attractions.description: Rated the #1 museum in the US by TripAdvisor for multiple years. An immersive, multi-pavilion complex covering the entire American experience in World War II with authentic artifacts, oral histories, and a 4D film experience. Budget at least 4 hours.

attractions.name: Frenchmen Street Live Music
attractions.description: The authentic local alternative to Bourbon Street, where bars and clubs line several blocks with live jazz, brass bands, and funk starting in the afternoon and going until the early hours. No cover at most venues; simply walk in and soak up the scene.
attractions.entranceFee: Free or small cover ($5-$10) at some venues

roadTripDays.tips: Cajun and Creole cuisine in New Orleans is a highlight of the trip; must-try spots include Cafe Du Monde (beignets), Dooky Chase, Commander's Palace, and Central Grocery (muffulettas)

hotels.name: Mid-range hotel in Pensacola or Pensacola Beach, FL
hotels.notes: Search Booking.com for current availability

attractions.name: Gulf Islands National Seashore (Fort Pickens)
attractions.description: A preserved stretch of the Gulf Coast's finest barrier island beaches within a national seashore. Fort Pickens is a 19th-century masonry fort where Apache leader Geronimo was imprisoned. Pristine sugar-white beaches with emerald green water.
attractions.entranceFee: $25 per vehicle (7-day pass); covers national seashore sites

attractions.name: Pensacola Beach
attractions.description: Consistently ranked among the best beaches in the US for its brilliant white quartz sand and turquoise water. The beach sits on Santa Rosa Island and offers calm Gulf waters ideal for swimming. Quieter and less commercialized than Destin.

attractions.name: Historic Pensacola Village
attractions.description: A collection of 19th-century historic buildings in downtown Pensacola preserving the city's Spanish, French, and British colonial heritage. Includes the T.T. Wentworth Jr. Florida State Museum and the Colonial Archaeological Trail.

roadTripDays.tips: In Florida, restaurant prices near theme parks and tourist beaches carry a significant premium; venture one or two blocks off the main tourist strip for better value

hotels.name: Mid-range hotel in Destin, FL
hotels.notes: Search Booking.com for current availability

attractions.name: Scenic Highway 30A Beach Towns
attractions.description: A 24-mile stretch of Highway 30A connecting planned beach towns including Seaside (inspiration for The Truman Show), Rosemary Beach, WaterColor, and Grayton Beach. White sand, turquoise water, and distinctive coastal architecture.

attractions.name: Destin Harbor and Crab Island
attractions.description: Destin is home to one of the world's luckiest fishing fleets and a shallow sandbar called Crab Island where boats anchor and people swim, socialize, and eat from floating food vendors. Snorkel tours depart from Harbor Walk Village.
attractions.hours: Harbor open daily; Crab Island best on summer weekends
attractions.entranceFee: Free to access; boat rentals $150-$300/half day

attractions.name: Henderson Beach State Park
attractions.description: Unspoiled stretch of Destin beachfront within a state park, protecting pristine dunes and wildlife away from the commercial strip. One of Florida's most beautiful beaches. Sunrise is spectacular here.

roadTripDays.tips: Book New Orleans swamp tours in advance; the best depart early morning when wildlife is most active

roadTripDays.to: Tampa via Tallahassee and Crystal River

hotels.name: The Godfrey Hotel & Cabanas Tampa
hotels.notes: Waterfront on Old Tampa Bay; outdoor pool and marina

hotels.name: Tampa Marriott Water Street
hotels.notes: Upscale downtown location near Amalie Arena

hotels.name: Armature Works Area Airbnb/VRBO
hotels.notes: Many vacation rentals in the Heights neighborhood

attractions.name: Wakulla Springs State Park
attractions.description: One of the world's largest and deepest freshwater springs, near Tallahassee, used as a location for the original Tarzan films and Creature from the Black Lagoon. Glass-bottom boat tours reveal the crystal-clear spring and abundant wildlife.

attractions.name: Crystal River National Wildlife Refuge
attractions.description: The only federally protected refuge created specifically for the West Indian manatee. Spring-fed Kings Bay maintains a constant 72 degrees, attracting manatees year-round. Kayak and snorkel tours available to swim with manatees.
attractions.hours: Refuge open daily, dawn to dusk
attractions.entranceFee: Free to access; guided tours $30-$60 per person

attractions.name: Ybor City Historic District, Tampa
attractions.description: Tampa's Latin Quarter, once the cigar capital of the world. Red-brick streets lined with historic cigar factories turned restaurants and clubs, with a thriving nightlife scene. The Columbia Restaurant (1905) is the oldest restaurant in Florida.
attractions.hours: Open daily; restaurants from 11am, nightlife from 9pm

roadTripDays.tips: Louisiana state law requires all front-seat passengers to wear seatbelts; Florida enforces hands-free phone laws while driving

roadTripDays.overnight: Tampa or St. Pete Beach, FL

hotels.name: Mid-range hotel in Tampa or St. Pete Beach, FL
hotels.notes: Search Booking.com for current availability

attractions.name: Clearwater Beach
attractions.description: Consistently voted America's best beach, Clearwater Beach offers 35 blocks of powdery white sand, calm turquoise water, a lively pier for sunset celebrations, and excellent seafood restaurants along the waterfront.

attractions.name: Florida Aquarium
attractions.description: A 250,000-square-foot aquarium in downtown Tampa featuring a no-touch shark exhibit, Coral Reef Gallery, and daily dive shows. One of the top aquariums in the Southeast.

attractions.name: Busch Gardens Tampa (optional theme park day)
attractions.description: An African-themed adventure park combining world-class roller coasters with a 300-acre animal preserve home to more than 200 species including white rhinos, gorillas, and cheetahs. Good alternative to Orlando for theme park fans.

hotels.name: Mid-range hotel in Orlando, FL
hotels.notes: Search Booking.com for current availability

attractions.name: Walt Disney World or Universal Orlando
attractions.description: Orlando's world-famous theme parks. Walt Disney World spans 27,000 acres with four main parks. Universal Orlando features Wizarding World of Harry Potter across two parks. Both require dedicated full days. Purchase tickets online weeks in advance.
attractions.hours: Parks typically open 9am-9pm (hours vary by season and park)
attractions.entranceFee: Disney: $109-$209 per day adults (date-based pricing); Universal: $110-$160 per day adults

attractions.name: Kennedy Space Center Visitor Complex
attractions.description: A 45-minute drive east of Orlando, on Merritt Island, this is the launch site for NASA's most significant missions. See Shuttle Atlantis up close, tour launch facilities, and watch rockets launch from the observation gantry (launch schedule varies).
attractions.hours: Open daily 9am-6pm; closed some launch days

attractions.name: Everglades National Park (alternate end)
attractions.description: If routing via Miami instead of Orlando, detour to Everglades National Park for airboat tours through sawgrass prairies, alligator sightings, and the only subtropical wilderness in North America. About 2.5 hours south of Tampa.

roadTripDays.to: Dallas via I-10 (or fly home from Orlando/Tampa)

hotels.name: Mid-range hotel in Houston, TX or Dallas, TX
hotels.notes: Search Booking.com for current availability

attractions.name: San Antonio River Walk and the Alamo
attractions.description: On the return through Texas, San Antonio is a must-stop. The River Walk is a 15-mile network of walkways along the San Antonio River lined with restaurants and bars. The Alamo, a 1718 Spanish mission and Texas independence site, is free to visit.
attractions.hours: River Walk open 24 hours; Alamo open daily 9am-5:30pm

attractions.name: Austin Food and Music Scene
attractions.description: The live music capital of the world with more music venues per capita than anywhere else. Sixth Street and Rainey Street offer bars and restaurants. Franklin Barbecue is legendary (arrive before 9am and expect a 2-hour wait; worth it).
attractions.hours: 6th Street music starts 9pm; most restaurants open 11am-10pm

attractions.name: Padre Island National Seashore
attractions.description: The world's longest undeveloped barrier island stretches 70 miles along the Texas Gulf Coast near Corpus Christi. Sea turtle nesting site in summer (June-August). Nearly empty beaches and excellent bird watching on the return route.

name: Scottish Highlands & Ireland
subtitle: Edinburgh to Dublin — Castles, Cliffs & Whisky

premium.label: Premium
premium.includes: Country house hotels, castle dinners, private tours; ~£310/€350/day

transportNotes: Drive on the LEFT. Open-jaw flights recommended (fly into EDI, out of DUB). Car rental required — book in advance. International Driving Permit needed for Republic of Ireland.
highlights: Visit Edinburgh Castle and the Scotch Whisky Experience on the Royal Mile
Drive through the iconic Glencoe valley and along the shores of Loch Ness
Spend two days on the Isle of Skye: Fairy Pools, Old Man of Storr, and Eilean Donan Castle
Explore Belfast's world-class Titanic museum and the Victorian Crown Liquor Saloon
Walk the UNESCO-listed Giant's Causeway and cross the Carrick-a-Rede Rope Bridge
Drive the Ring of Kerry with views of Skellig Michael and dramatic Atlantic coastline
End in Dublin: Trinity College's Book of Kells, Guinness Storehouse, and Temple Bar pub culture

hotels.name: The Balmoral Hotel
hotels.notes: Iconic landmark at Princes Street; splurge-worthy

hotels.name: Grassmarket Hotel
hotels.notes: Boutique in Old Town, great pub scene downstairs

hotels.name: Tune Hotel Edinburgh
hotels.notes: Budget-friendly, clean, central location

attractions.name: Royal Mile and Old Town
attractions.description: The spine of Edinburgh's medieval Old Town connecting Edinburgh Castle at the top to the Palace of Holyroodhouse at the bottom. Lined with closes (alleyways), tartan shops, whisky bars, and historic buildings. Free to walk.

attractions.name: Princes Street Gardens and Calton Hill
attractions.description: A beautifully landscaped garden beneath the castle rock in the heart of the city. Calton Hill offers some of the best views of Edinburgh's skyline with the Scott Monument, Holyrood Park, and the Firth of Forth visible on clear days.
attractions.hours: Gardens open daily, dawn to dusk; Calton Hill accessible 24 hours

attractions.name: National Museum of Scotland
attractions.description: Five floors of Scottish history and culture covering everything from ancient Pictish stones to Dolly the cloned sheep. One of the finest national museums in the UK and completely free to enter.

tripDays.tips: Drive on the LEFT in both the UK and Ireland; automatic transmission must be specifically requested when booking as manuals are the default in Europe

hotels.name: The Balmoral Hotel
hotels.notes: Iconic landmark at Princes Street; splurge-worthy

hotels.name: Grassmarket Hotel
hotels.notes: Boutique in Old Town, great pub scene downstairs

hotels.name: Tune Hotel Edinburgh
hotels.notes: Budget-friendly, clean, central location

attractions.name: Edinburgh Castle
attractions.description: Scotland's most iconic landmark, perched on a volcanic plug 250 feet above the city. Home to the Scottish Crown Jewels, the Stone of Destiny, the Great Hall, and the One O'Clock Gun fired daily (except Sundays). Book tickets well in advance.
attractions.hours: Summer (Apr-Sep): 9:30am-6pm; Winter (Oct-Mar): 9:30am-5pm; closed Christmas Day and Boxing Day
attractions.entranceFee: GBP 23.50 adults online / GBP 26 walk-up; children GBP 14 online

attractions.name: Scotch Whisky Experience
attractions.description: Located at the top of the Royal Mile next to the Castle, this immersive attraction takes visitors through all five Scotch whisky regions using a barrel ride, tasting room, and one of the world's largest private whisky collections (3,384 bottles). Ideal for whisky newcomers and enthusiasts alike.
attractions.entranceFee: Silver tour: GBP 20 adults; Gold tour with additional tasting: GBP 40

attractions.name: Palace of Holyroodhouse
attractions.description: The official Scottish residence of the British Royal Family, at the foot of the Royal Mile. Visit the state apartments, Mary Queen of Scots' chambers, and the ruined 12th-century Holyrood Abbey on the grounds. Connects to Holyrood Park and the trail to Arthur's Seat.

tripDays.tips: The Highland roads (A82, A87) are predominantly single-track with passing places; use them correctly and do not park in passing places

hotels.name: Clachaig Inn
hotels.notes: Classic Highland pub-inn, favorite of hillwalkers

hotels.name: Kingshouse Hotel
hotels.notes: Remotely spectacular; inside Glencoe valley

attractions.name: Stirling Castle
attractions.description: One of Scotland's grandest and most historically important castles, crowning a volcanic plug above Stirling. The royal court of Mary Queen of Scots operated here, and it overlooks the site of the Battle of Bannockburn (1314). A worthwhile stop en route north.

attractions.name: Rannoch Moor
attractions.description: A vast, desolate Highland moorland stretching 50 square miles between Pitlochry and Glencoe. The road through it (A82) offers haunting views of lochs, peat bogs, and distant Munros. Pull over at the designated viewpoints for photographs.

attractions.name: Glencoe Valley
attractions.description: One of the most dramatic landscapes in the British Isles: a glacially carved valley flanked by the Three Sisters of Bidean nam Bian and the peaks of Buachaille Etive Mor. Site of the 1692 Glencoe Massacre of the MacDonald clan. Visitor center tells the full history. Hiking trails from easy to strenuous.

tripDays.tips: Scotland weather is famously changeable year-round: pack waterproof layers regardless of season, especially for outdoor hiking on Skye and in Glencoe

hotels.name: Hotel in Isle of Skye (Portree), Scotland
hotels.notes: Book well in advance — popular destination

attractions.name: Eilean Donan Castle
attractions.description: Scotland's most photographed castle, sitting on a tidal island where three lochs meet, connected to the mainland by a graceful stone bridge. The current structure was mostly rebuilt in the early 20th century. The backdrop for the Highlander film.

attractions.name: Skye Bridge and Kyleakin
attractions.description: Cross the Skye Bridge to arrive on the island at Kyleakin village, with views of Castle Moil, a 15th-century ruin on the shore. A scenic introduction to the island.

attractions.name: Old Man of Storr
attractions.description: A distinctive rock pinnacle rising from the Trotternish Ridge, accessible via a 4km (2.5 mile) return hike from a staffed car park. One of the most iconic walks in Scotland with views across the Inner Sound to the mainland. Boots and waterproofs essential.
attractions.hours: Accessible 24 hours; car park staffed in season
attractions.entranceFee: Car park charge: GBP 5-8; walk free

tripDays.tips: The Isle of Skye is extremely popular July-August; accommodation books out 4-6 months in advance and roads can be congested by 10am

tripDays.travelTime: Driving between Skye attractions (40 minutes total)

hotels.name: Hotel in Isle of Skye (Portree), Scotland
hotels.notes: Book well in advance — popular destination

attractions.name: Fairy Pools, Glenbrittle
attractions.description: A series of crystal-clear, turquoise-blue plunge pools formed by waterfalls descending from the Black Cuillin mountains. The 2.5-mile return walk leads through dramatic mountain scenery. Popular; arrive early or at dusk to avoid crowds.
attractions.hours: Accessible 24 hours; car park operates Apr-Oct

attractions.name: Portree Harbour and Quiraing
attractions.description: Portree is Skye's charming capital with a candy-colored harbor, seafood restaurants, and the base for island exploration. The Quiraing is a dramatic landslip on the Trotternish Peninsula, accessible by a 4.5-mile circular walk with truly otherworldly scenery.

attractions.name: Dunvegan Castle and Gardens
attractions.description: The oldest continuously inhabited castle in Scotland (since the 13th century) and seat of the Chiefs of Clan MacLeod. Houses the Fairy Flag, believed to have magical powers. Loch-side gardens with views to the Outer Hebrides.

tripDays.tips: Book Edinburgh Castle, Titanic Belfast, Book of Kells, and Guinness Storehouse tickets online before you travel to guarantee entry and save money

tripDays.location: Inverness, Scotland (or fly to Belfast same day)
tripDays.overnight: Inverness, Scotland (or fly to Belfast same day)

hotels.name: Hotel in Inverness, Scotland (or fly to Belfast same day)
hotels.notes: Book well in advance — popular destination

attractions.name: Loch Ness and Urquhart Castle
attractions.description: The famous loch holds more water than all freshwater lakes in England and Wales combined. Urquhart Castle, a ruined 13th-century fortress on the loch shore, is one of Scotland's most visited sites and offers panoramic views of the water where monster sightings are claimed.
attractions.hours: Urquhart Castle open daily Apr-Sep 9:30am-6pm; Oct-Mar 9:30am-4:30pm
attractions.entranceFee: Urquhart Castle: GBP 11 adults, GBP 6.50 children

attractions.name: The Loch Ness Experience Centre, Drumnadrochit
attractions.description: An interactive exhibition using sonar data, historical records, and scientific research to explore the myth and reality of the Loch Ness Monster. A fun and informative 90-minute stop.

attractions.name: Inverness Victorian Market and Riverside
attractions.description: Scotland's Highland capital with a Victorian indoor market hall, the River Ness, and a restored 19th-century castle (currently under renovation). A relaxing base before the flight to Belfast.

tripDays.tips: Scotland's Responsible Access Law (Land Reform Act 2003) grants the right to roam on most land; you can hike across private farmland and moorland with no restrictions if you behave responsibly

tripDays.travelTime: Short flight approx. 1 hour; ferry option 3 hours drive + 2-hour ferry

hotels.name: Hotel Bullitt Belfast
hotels.notes: Stylish boutique in Cathedral Quarter

hotels.name: Titanic Hotel Belfast
hotels.notes: Stay in the original Titanic drawing offices

hotels.name: Holiday Inn Express Belfast City
hotels.notes: Budget reliable near the Titanic Quarter

attractions.name: Titanic Belfast Museum
attractions.description: The world's largest Titanic visitor experience, built in the shape of the ship's prow on the exact slipways where RMS Titanic was constructed. Nine interactive galleries covering the Titanic's design, construction, launch, and sinking. One of the best museums in Ireland.
attractions.hours: Open daily Apr-May 9am-6pm; Jun-Aug 9am-7pm (last entry 90 min before closing)
attractions.entranceFee: GBP 24.95 adults, GBP 11 children ages 5-15; under 5 free

attractions.name: Belfast Cathedral Quarter and St. Anne's Cathedral
attractions.description: Belfast's artistic heart, filled with independent restaurants, craft beer bars, street art, and the Black Box performance venue. St. Anne's Cathedral has a famous mosaic ceiling and the tomb of Lord Carson. A vibrant evening neighborhood.

attractions.name: The Crown Liquor Saloon
attractions.description: A Victorian gin palace on Great Victoria Street, now owned by the National Trust, with ornate ceramic tiles, carved woodwork, and gas lighting. Dating to 1885, it is arguably the most beautiful pub in Ireland. A mandatory stop.
attractions.entranceFee: Free to enter; drinks at pub prices

tripDays.tips: In Ireland, driving distances look short on a map but narrow country roads mean 40 miles can take 1.5 hours; add buffer time to all driving estimates

hotels.name: Hotel Bullitt Belfast
hotels.notes: Stylish boutique in Cathedral Quarter

hotels.name: Titanic Hotel Belfast
hotels.notes: Stay in the original Titanic drawing offices

hotels.name: Holiday Inn Express Belfast City
hotels.notes: Budget reliable near the Titanic Quarter

attractions.name: Giant's Causeway
attractions.description: Northern Ireland's only UNESCO World Heritage Site: approximately 40,000 interlocking basalt columns formed by ancient volcanic activity, creating perfectly hexagonal stepping stones into the sea. Surrounded by coastal cliffs and sea stacks. The coastline itself is free to access at any hour; the National Trust Visitor Centre charges separately.
attractions.hours: Causeway: open dawn to dusk (free access always). Visitor Centre: Jun-Aug 9am-7pm; Mar-May and Sep-Oct 9am-6pm; Nov-Feb 10am-4pm
attractions.entranceFee: Visitor Centre: GBP 14 adults online, GBP 7.50 children; causeway access is always free

attractions.name: Carrick-a-Rede Rope Bridge
attractions.description: A 20-meter rope bridge spanning a chasm 30 meters above the sea, originally erected by salmon fishermen. Crossing provides dramatic views of the Antrim coastline and Rathlin Island. Book in advance as tickets sell out.
attractions.entranceFee: GBP 13 adults, GBP 6.50 children (National Trust; members free)

attractions.name: Old Bushmills Distillery
attractions.description: The world's oldest licensed whiskey distillery (1608), located a few miles from the Giant's Causeway. Tours of the working distillery end with a tasting of multiple expressions. Uniquely, Bushmills makes Irish whiskey (triple-distilled, no peat) rather than Scotch.
attractions.entranceFee: Classic tour: GBP 15 adults; premium tours from GBP 25

tripDays.tips: The Ring of Kerry is officially driven counterclockwise to prevent coach bus collisions on the narrow roads

tripDays.travelTime: 3 hours (Belfast to Killarney direct via M1/N7)

hotels.name: Hotel in Killarney, County Kerry, Ireland
hotels.notes: Book well in advance — popular destination

attractions.name: Dublin quick stop: Trinity College and Book of Kells
attractions.description: Ireland's most visited paid attraction, housed in the 1712 Old Library of Trinity College Dublin. Two 9th-century illuminated gospel manuscripts and the breathtaking Long Room housing 200,000 ancient books. Book timed entry tickets well in advance in summer.
attractions.entranceFee: From EUR 19 (Book of Kells and Old Library only) to EUR 26 (full experience including digital exhibition)

attractions.name: Killarney National Park
attractions.description: Ireland's oldest and largest national park, encompassing Killarney's three lakes, McGillycuddy's Reeks (Ireland's highest mountains), and Killarney town. The Ross Castle ruins on the lake shore and Muckross House estate are highlights. Cycle or take a jaunting car (horse-drawn cart).
attractions.hours: Park open 24 hours; Muckross House Apr-Oct daily 9am-5:30pm
attractions.entranceFee: Park free; Muckross House EUR 9 adults

attractions.name: Killarney Town Pubs
attractions.description: Killarney is a lively base town with excellent trad music sessions in its pubs. Hannigans, O'Connors, and The Laurels are reliable venues for live traditional Irish music most evenings from 9pm.

tripDays.tips: Ferries between Scotland and Northern Ireland: Stena Line runs Cairnryan to Belfast (2 hours 15 min by fast ferry); P&O runs Cairnryan to Larne (2 hours). Book in advance

tripDays.travelTime: 4 hours pure driving; 7-8 hours with stops

hotels.name: Hotel in Killarney, County Kerry, Ireland
hotels.notes: Book well in advance — popular destination

attractions.name: Skellig Ring and Kerry Cliffs
attractions.description: A detour off the main Ring of Kerry route via Portmagee and Valentia Island, leading to the Kerry Cliffs with 820-foot sheer drops and views of Skellig Michael, the UNESCO-listed early Christian monastery island used as a filming location for Star Wars. Boat tours to Skellig Michael depart seasonally from Portmagee.
attractions.hours: Kerry Cliffs open daily 9am-6pm; Skellig boat tours May-Sept weather permitting
attractions.entranceFee: Kerry Cliffs EUR 6 adults; Skellig Michael landing EUR 15 (boat tour EUR 60-80 extra)

attractions.name: Derrynane House and Beach
attractions.description: The ancestral home of Daniel O'Connell, 'The Liberator' who won Catholic Emancipation in 1829. The house museum is beautifully set in natural woodland above a stunning Blue Flag beach and tidal islands. One of the highlights of the Ring of Kerry.
attractions.entranceFee: EUR 5 adults; beach and grounds free

attractions.name: Ladies View and the Killarney Lakes
attractions.description: A panoramic viewpoint on the N71 offering what Queen Victoria's ladies-in-waiting called the finest view in Ireland: Upper Lake, MacGillycuddy's Reeks, and Killarney Valley. A short roadside stop that delivers maximum drama.

tripDays.tips: Both the UK and Ireland use the pound and euro respectively; airport exchange rates are poor so use a no-foreign-transaction-fee credit card or withdraw cash at in-country ATMs

tripDays.travelTime: 3 hours direct; 5 hours via Cliffs of Moher

hotels.name: The Merrion Hotel
hotels.notes: Five-star Georgian townhouse near Trinity College

hotels.name: The Marker Hotel
hotels.notes: Modern design hotel in Grand Canal Dock

hotels.name: Generator Dublin
hotels.notes: Stylish hostel with private rooms; great social scene

attractions.name: Cliffs of Moher (via N67/R478)
attractions.description: The most visited natural attraction in Ireland: sea cliffs rising 702 feet above the Atlantic, stretching 8 miles along the Clare coast. The O'Brien's Tower viewpoint is iconic. Arrive early to beat the crowds and get clear photographs. The coastal walk north from the visitor center is spectacular.
attractions.hours: Open daily; visitor center 9am-7pm summer, 9am-5pm off-season
attractions.entranceFee: EUR 8 adults, EUR 4 children; includes car parking

attractions.name: Dublin's Temple Bar and Grafton Street
attractions.description: Temple Bar is Dublin's cultural quarter with pubs, street performers, restaurants, and galleries. The traditional Irish pub The Brazen Head (est. 1198) and Mulligan's are institutions. Grafton Street is a pedestrian shopping street with Bewley's Oriental Cafe and street musicians.
attractions.hours: Temple Bar pubs open from noon; Grafton Street shops 9am-6pm

attractions.name: Guinness Storehouse
attractions.description: The most visited paid attraction in all of Ireland, built in a converted 1904 fermentation building at the original St. James's Gate Brewery. Seven floors of Guinness history and advertising art, ending with a pint poured by yourself in the Gravity Bar with 360-degree views of Dublin.
attractions.entranceFee: EUR 28 adults online; under 13 not admitted to bar floor

hotels.name: Hotel in Fly home
hotels.notes: Book well in advance — popular destination

attractions.name: Trinity College Campus (morning visit)
attractions.description: Walk the cobbled squares of Ireland's oldest university (1592) early in the morning before the crowds arrive. The Book of Kells Experience opens at 8:30am; book the first slot for an uncrowded visit to the Long Room.
attractions.hours: Campus open daily; Book of Kells from 8:30am
attractions.entranceFee: Campus free; Book of Kells from EUR 19

attractions.name: St. Stephen's Green and Merrion Square
attractions.description: Dublin's two finest Georgian squares. St. Stephen's Green is a 22-acre park in the city center. Merrion Square is surrounded by colorful Georgian townhouses and features a relaxed park with Oscar Wilde's statue and weekend art market.

name: I-80 / I-70 Fast Route
subtitle: Chicago to San Francisco — The Direct Transcontinental Highway
bestMonths: April–October (avoid peak summer heat in desert sections)

budget.label: Budget
budget.includes: budget motels, fast food + 1 sit-down, gas shared between travelers

midrange.label: Mid-range
midrange.includes: mid-range hotels, casual restaurants, fuel, some paid attractions

premium.label: Premium
premium.includes: boutique hotels, nice restaurants, premium experiences

transportNotes: Drive your own car. 350-400 miles/day maximum. Gas ~$3.85/gal (AAA avg).
highlights: Rocky Mountain NP
Bonneville Salt Flats
Lake Tahoe
Golden Gate Bridge

hotels.name: Graduate Iowa City
hotels.notes: Hip hotel near University of Iowa campus; literary/academic theme; great bar

hotels.name: Hampton Inn Iowa City
hotels.notes: Solid mid-range; free breakfast; near I-80

hotels.name: Hilton Garden Inn Iowa City Downtown University
hotels.notes: Modern; downtown location; close to pedestrian mall

attractions.name: Starved Rock State Park (brief detour)
attractions.location: Utica, IL (90 miles SW of Chicago)
attractions.description: 18 canyons carved by glacial meltwater waterfalls, towering sandstone buttes, and eagle viewing along the Illinois River. One of Illinois' premier natural attractions — worth a 2-hour stop before crossing into Iowa.

attractions.name: Quad Cities (Davenport/Bettendorf, IA & Rock Island/Moline, IL)
attractions.description: The Mississippi River crossing — the Centennial Bridge and Government Bridge offer great views. Rock Island Arsenal (largest US Army arsenal) has a free museum. Downtown Davenport has a historic riverfront.
attractions.hours: Rock Island Arsenal Museum Mon-Fri 10am-4pm; riverfront always accessible

attractions.name: Iowa City Pedestrian Mall
attractions.description: College town pedestrian district with excellent independent bookstores (Prairie Lights), live music venues, and diverse restaurants. Home of the University of Iowa Writers' Workshop — the oldest in the country.
attractions.hours: Businesses typically 10am-9pm; outdoor mall always accessible

attractions.name: Herbert Hoover National Historic Site
attractions.description: Birthplace and presidential library of the 31st president. The tiny 14x20-foot cottage where Hoover was born in 1874 is preserved on the Hoover Prairie. Library has excellent exhibits on his humanitarian work.

hotels.name: Fort Kearny Inn
hotels.notes: Comfortable; near Kearney attractions; good value

hotels.name: Holiday Inn Express Kearney
hotels.notes: Reliable chain; pool; good breakfast

hotels.name: Hilton Garden Inn Kearney
hotels.notes: Best mid-range option in Kearney; fitness center

attractions.name: Pottawattamie County Squirrel Cage Jail
attractions.description: One of only three rotating cylindrical 'squirrel cage' jails ever built (1885) — a mechanical marvel where a single guard could see all 3 tiers of cells by rotating the structure. Now a museum.

attractions.name: The Durham Museum (Union Station)
attractions.description: Housed in Omaha's magnificent 1931 Art Deco Union Station — one of the finest train stations in the US. History museum with Pullman railroad cars, a working soda fountain, and Great Plains exhibits.

attractions.name: Platte River Valley Pony Express Trail
attractions.description: The I-80 corridor follows the original Pony Express and Oregon Trail routes through the Platte River Valley. The Great Platte River Road Archway Monument in Kearney spans the interstate itself with exhibits on westward migration.

attractions.name: Chimney Rock National Historic Site (detour)
attractions.description: The most recognizable landmark on the Oregon Trail — a 325-foot spire of clay and sandstone that westward emigrants noted in their journals more than any other landmark. Short drive off I-80.

hotels.name: The Crawford Hotel (Union Station)
hotels.notes: Inside Denver's spectacular 1881 Union Station; stunning historic building; bar and restaurants on premises

hotels.name: Comfort Inn Denver East (near I-70)
hotels.notes: Budget-friendly; convenient for I-70 continuation next morning

hotels.name: Residence Inn Denver Downtown
hotels.notes: Extended-stay amenities; good downtown location; kitchen suites available

attractions.name: Rocky Mountain National Park (detour — highly recommended)
attractions.location: Estes Park, CO (75 miles north of Denver via US-36)
attractions.description: Trail Ridge Road (US-34) is the highest continuous paved road in the US at 12,183 feet — a 48-mile alpine highway above treeline with elk, marmots, and sweeping Rocky Mountain views. Timed entry required in summer (book at recreation.gov).
attractions.hours: Park open 24 hours; Trail Ridge Road typically May-Oct; $35 per vehicle (7 days); timed entry permit required May 26-Oct 11 (2026)

attractions.name: Denver Art Museum
attractions.description: One of the largest art museums between Chicago and LA, housed in a spectacular Daniel Libeskind titanium building. World-class Native American, pre-Columbian, and modern/contemporary collections.
attractions.hours: Tue-Sun 10am-5pm; $22 adults; free to CO residents Sat

attractions.name: Red Rocks Amphitheatre & Park
attractions.location: Morrison, CO (15 miles west of Denver)
attractions.description: Natural 9,000-seat amphitheater between two 300-foot red sandstone monoliths. Even without a show, the geological formations, Colorado scenery, and the trading post museum make it worth a 2-hour stop.
attractions.hours: Daily 5am-11pm; free admission to park; events ticketed

attractions.name: Denver's RiNo Arts District & Union Station
attractions.description: The River North Arts District has 300+ murals and a thriving craft brewery scene. Denver Union Station (1881, beautifully restored) anchors the Lower Downtown (LoDo) neighborhood with restaurants and the Terminal Bar.
attractions.hours: Union Station open daily 5am-11pm; neighborhood businesses vary

hotels.name: Doubletree by Hilton Grand Junction
hotels.notes: Best full-service hotel in town; pool; near downtown

hotels.name: Hampton Inn Grand Junction
hotels.notes: Reliable; free breakfast; near Colorado National Monument entrance

hotels.name: Hotel Maverick at Colorado Mesa University
hotels.notes: Modern boutique hotel; unique university setting; excellent restaurant

attractions.name: Colorado National Monument
attractions.description: A spectacular 23-mile Rim Rock Drive winds through red rock canyons with formations up to 2,000 feet high. Window Rock, Balanced Rock, Coke Ovens, and Independence Monument are highlights. Far less crowded than Utah parks.
attractions.hours: Park open 24 hours; Visitor Center 9am-5pm; $25 per vehicle (7 days)

attractions.name: Glenwood Canyon (I-70 scenic drive)
attractions.description: The 12.5-mile stretch of I-70 through Glenwood Canyon — considered one of the most spectacular interstate highway segments in the world, carved through 2,000-foot granite walls along the Colorado River. No stopping needed — just drive.

attractions.name: Glenwood Springs Hot Springs (brief detour)
attractions.description: The world's largest natural hot spring pool — a 405-foot outdoor pool fed by 3.5 million gallons of 90°F water daily. A perfect mid-trip rest stop after hard driving.

hotels.name: Whitney Peak Hotel
hotels.notes: Non-gaming boutique hotel in downtown Reno; world's tallest climbing wall on the exterior; excellent value

hotels.name: Eldorado Resort Casino Reno
hotels.notes: Classic Reno casino-hotel; downtown location; multiple restaurants

hotels.name: Courtyard by Marriott Reno Downtown
hotels.notes: Modern; no casino; good choice for non-gamblers

attractions.name: Salt Lake City Center (brief stop)
attractions.description: Temple Square and the Utah State Capitol are worth a 2-hour stop. The 10-acre Temple Square has the famous Salt Lake Temple and Tabernacle. The Capitol offers panoramic city views against the Wasatch Mountains.
attractions.hours: Temple Square grounds daily; Capitol Mon-Fri 8am-8pm, Sat-Sun 8am-6pm

attractions.name: Bonneville Salt Flats
attractions.description: The vast white salt flat where land speed records have been broken since 1914. Turn off I-80 at Exit 4 near Wendover — drive or walk onto the flats for a surreal 360-degree flat horizon experience. Free BLM land.
attractions.hours: Open daily (BLM land); no fee; check for seasonal closures after rain

attractions.name: Great Basin National Park (optional detour — 2 hrs south on US-93)
attractions.description: Nevada's most underrated park — Wheeler Peak (13,063 feet), Lehman Caves limestone caverns with rare cave shields, and ancient bristlecone pines up to 5,000 years old. A major detour but spectacular.
attractions.hours: Park open 24 hours; Lehman Caves tours daily 9am-4pm; $10/person cave tour

attractions.name: Reno Downtown & Riverwalk
attractions.description: Reno's downtown Truckee River Riverwalk is surprisingly charming — galleries, restaurants, and whitewater kayaking in an urban river park. The National Automobile Museum has an outstanding collection of 200+ cars.
attractions.hours: Riverwalk always accessible; Auto Museum Mon-Sat 9:30am-5:30pm, Sun 10am-4pm; $15 adults

hotels.name: Hotel Zephyr San Francisco
hotels.notes: Nautical-themed hotel at Fisherman's Wharf; walkable to Pier 39 and bay views

hotels.name: Hyatt Regency San Francisco
hotels.notes: Iconic atrium lobby; excellent Embarcadero location; stunning views

hotels.name: Hotel Nikko San Francisco
hotels.notes: Union Square location; good value for SF; Japanese aesthetic

attractions.name: Lake Tahoe (highly recommended detour — US-50 south or I-80 via Truckee)
attractions.description: The largest alpine lake in North America — impossibly clear blue water surrounded by pine forests and Sierra Nevada peaks. Drive the scenic CA-89 around the lake. Sand Harbor Beach on the Nevada side is spectacular. Only 30-60 minute detour from I-80 via Truckee.
attractions.hours: Always accessible; sand beaches typically free; state parks $8-15 per vehicle

attractions.name: Donner Memorial State Park
attractions.description: Commemorates the tragic 1846-47 Donner Party stranded in the Sierra Nevada. The museum is sobering and historically fascinating. The snow pillar shows 22-foot snow depth that winter.

attractions.name: Golden Gate Bridge & Vista Point
attractions.description: The iconic 1937 suspension bridge. Walk or bike across (3.4 miles round trip, free). Vista Point (Marin side) has the classic postcard view from the north. Baker Beach offers views from below on the San Francisco side.
attractions.hours: Bridge open 24 hours for pedestrians/bikes; visitor plaza daily 9am-6pm

attractions.name: Fisherman's Wharf & Pier 39
attractions.description: Sea lions at Pier 39, sourdough bread bowls, fresh Dungeness crab from the waterfront vendors. The historic Cannery and Ghirardelli Square are steps away. Classic San Francisco arrival experience.

attractions.name: Alcatraz Island
attractions.description: The former federal penitentiary — take the ferry from Pier 33 for the acclaimed audio tour narrated by former guards and inmates. Book well in advance; day tours often sell out 2+ weeks ahead.
attractions.hours: Ferries daily 9am-3:55pm (day); evening tours also available; $44 adults (day tour, includes ferry)
